user.title: Zero Net Export
user.description: Create the Zero Net Export backend entry first, then finish setup from Home Assistant's normal Configure flow. Runtime defaults start in a safe bootstrap state until you map sources and add devices.

data_description.name: This is just the label Home Assistant will show for this Zero Net Export system. Pick something human-friendly, like the site, inverter, or switchboard name. You can finish source mapping, refresh tuning, and device onboarding after entry creation from the integration's Configure screens.

menu_options.native_setup: Native setup, source mapping, and refresh interval

native_setup.title: Native setup and source mapping

data_description.grid_import_power_entity: Grid import power sensor. Use a signed split helper if your hardware exposes a single net-grid sensor.
data_description.grid_export_power_entity: Grid export power sensor. Use a signed split helper if your hardware exposes a single net-grid sensor.
data_description.battery_soc_entity: Optional battery SOC sensor for reserve-aware control.
data_description.refresh_seconds: How often the backend re-reads source sensors and re-plans device actions.

devices.title: Managed devices

data_description.device_inventory_json: Current practical native path for managed devices. Paste a JSON array of controllable devices here, then save to reload the integration.

panel.title: Optional Zero Net Export panel
panel.description:
The panel app is now optional. Normal setup should work from the native Configure flow and device-page diagnostics.

If you want the richer custom UI and it loads cleanly in your Home Assistant install, open the sidebar item **Zero Net Export** or browse directly to `{panel_path}`.

Integration: **{entry_title}**

advanced.title: Controller tuning
advanced.description: Adjust controller defaults and runtime tuning here. Use the native setup and managed-device steps for normal onboarding.

data_description.target_export_w: The export value the controller aims for during normal operation. Leave this at 0 W unless you intentionally want a small export or import bias.
data_description.deadband_w: How much error Zero Net Export will tolerate before changing devices. Higher values reduce device flapping; lower values chase the target more aggressively.
data_description.battery_reserve_soc: The minimum battery percentage to protect before discretionary surplus-absorption actions are allowed. Choose the lowest battery level you still want to keep in reserve.
data_description.refresh_seconds: How often the backend re-reads your source sensors and re-plans control actions. Shorter intervals react faster; longer intervals reduce churn.

error.device_inventory_invalid: The device inventory JSON is invalid or incomplete.
error.source_entities_invalid: One or more source entities are missing, duplicated, or invalid.

abort.panel_only: The panel app is optional. Return to Configure for native setup, managed devices, and tuning.
